**Envisioning the Cricket App**

I'm now fully immersed in the "Cricket Manager 26" app concept. My focus is on the core functionality, especially the integration of the Gemini API for intelligent game simulation and the selection of appropriate React components. I'm visualizing the user interface, aiming for a modern, intuitive design using Tailwind CSS. It's a challenging but exciting project, and I'm eager to delve into the coding.


**Expanding AI Capabilities**

I've been concentrating on integrating the Gemini API for more advanced functionalities within the app. Specifically, I'm working on an *AI Scout* feature that can analyze the current squad. This will allow the user to make data-driven decisions. The underlying components are starting to take shape, and I'm eager to build out the full user interface. I also want to make sure the game simulations are realistic and engaging.


**Adding Vivid Commentary**

The *AI Commentator* is now the primary focus. I'm exploring how Gemini can simulate the drama of a cricket match through dynamic, over-by-over narrative text. The visual design is taking shape, with deep greens, whites, and vibrant accent colors to reflect the high-energy nature of cricket. Modern, clean cards for data visualization, potentially using glassmorphism, are my goal. I'm building towards a real manager game experience, and I'm also focusing on defining the data structures for cricket stats.

import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { MatchResult, Inning, GameData } from '../types'; interface MatchResultScreenProps { result: MatchResult | null; onBack: () => void; userTeamId: string; gameData: GameData; } const InningBlock = ({ inning, color }: { inning: Inning, color: string }) => (

{inning.teamName}

Overs {inning.overs} {inning.score}/{inning.wickets}
Top Batters {inning.batting.sort((a,b)=>b.runs-a.runs).slice(0, 3).map(b => (
{b.playerName} {b.runs} ({b.balls})
))}
Top Bowlers {inning.bowling.sort((a,b)=>b.wickets-a.wickets).slice(0, 2).map(b => (
{b.playerName} {b.wickets}-{b.runsConceded} ({b.overs})
))}
); const MatchResultScreen: React.FC = ({ result, onBack, userTeamId, gameData }) => { if (!result) return
; const teamAData = gameData.allTeamsData.find(t => t.id === result.firstInning.teamId)!; const teamBData = gameData.allTeamsData.find(t => t.id === result.secondInning.teamId)!; return (
VS

{result.summary}

Final Result Official

Match Scorecard

Man of the Match {result.manOfTheMatch.playerName}
MVP
) }; export default MatchResultScreen;
